published_prior_art.cite: none; routine 2x2 odds-ratio algebra used only as a building block

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 is budgeted RD trimming and pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 is ordinal principal-strata LP; neither has Lee always-selected alpha-coupled Gamma-kappa frontier

published_prior_art.cite: Lee2009SampleSelection gives monotone trimming; ShepherdGilbertJemiaiRotnitzky2006/JemiaiRotnitzkyShepherdGilbert2007 give principal-stratum sensitivity; Okamoto2025RobustifyLee gives stochastic monotonicity/symmetry, not the coupled finite Gamma,kappa sharp frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: ManskiNonparametricBounds_Study records bounded-outcome selected-cell algebra only, not finite collider-dependence strict-tightening equality cases

published_prior_art.cite: Manski1990NonparametricBounds and HorowitzManski1995Censoring supply unrestricted mixture benchmarks; SmithVanderWeele2019Selection supplies outer selection-bias factors, not principal-strata equality-case classification
ShepherdGilbertJemiaiRotnitzky2006/JemiaiRotnitzkyShepherdGilbert2007 and HonoreHu2020Selection provide the named workflow comparators, but not observable Delta_SGJR/Delta_HH coupling thresholds

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Zhang, Rubin, and Mealli (2009, JASA) as an extra Job-Corps principal-stratification comparator, then state explicitly that its likelihood/principal-strata modelling is not a finite selected-mixture Gamma,kappa sharp-bound frontier.
For the numerical witness, compute the global alpha profile around alpha=0.3 and report whether the displayed local endpoint is also global or only a local separation certificate.
Strengthen Conjecture 3 by naming the exact finite-binary optimization dimension and constraints for the SGJR/JR and HH benchmark support problems in one compact displayed block.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 with Conjecture 1 as the flagship kernel and keep Conjecture 3 as the tier-supporting benchmark frontier.
